articles.title: RNA, AI and the end of one-size-fits-all nutrition
articles.summary: Forget one-size-fits-all wellness. RNA, AI and the microbiome are revealing a more personal path to healthier aging. Longevity science has revolved around a kind of genetic determinism. If we could just decode our DNA, the thinking went, we could predict and perhaps prevent the diseases of aging. However, as this week’s episode of Longevity.Technology UNLOCKED […] The post RNA, AI and the end of one-size-fits-all nutrition appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Stem cell pioneer launches in the United States
articles.summary: Newly validated pluripotent-like platform enters the US market, offering immune-tolerant alternative to traditional cell therapies. Patients seeking a particular kind of cellular therapy no longer need to cross an ocean for it. MuseCell Innovations (MCI) has announced its formal commercial launch within the United States, positioning itself as the exclusive global licensor of authentic Dezawa […] The post Stem cell pioneer launches in the United States appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Four Alzheimer’s companies to watch at AAIC 2026
articles.summary: As conversation expands beyond amyloid, upcoming conference highlights shift toward neurotech, regenerative medicine and practical home care. The Alzheimer’s Association International Conference (AAIC) has long been where the field reveals its next chapter. This year’s meeting, taking place 12–15 July in London, looks no different. While headline-grabbing drug approvals have reshaped Alzheimer’s care over the […] The post Four Alzheimer’s companies to watch at AAIC 2026 appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Laser treatment found to reverse skin aging markers
articles.summary: A cosmetic laser may be doing something science has struggled to achieve: changing the biology of aging skin, not just how it looks. A new human study published in Nature’s Scientific Reports has found that Candela’s Nordlys non-ablative fractional laser appears to reverse molecular changes associated with skin aging, offering what researchers describe as the […] The post Laser treatment found to reverse skin aging markers appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Biogen targets recurring revenue from neurology and rare diseases
articles.summary: Biogen is diversifying its portfolio across neurology and rare diseases to build long-term recurring revenue. The strategy focuses on high-value biologic therapies for central nervous system disorders, including multiple sclerosis and spinal muscular atrophy, and on specialty medicines for genetically defined rare diseases. The company claims its R&D-driven model combines internal discovery with collaborations and […] The post Biogen targets recurring revenue from neurology and rare diseases appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Leerink names Altimmune attractive in MASH therapeutics market
articles.summary: Leerink initiated coverage of Altimmune, Inc. on 22 June 2026, assigning an Outperform rating and a $10 price target and identifying the firm as an attractive play in the mash therapeutics market. The move followed 48-week results from Altimmune’s IMPACT Phase 2b trial announced in May 2026, evaluating pemvidutide in patients with metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. articles.title: AlzeCure signs $2.2b deal with QuantumCell to advance Alzheimer’s platform
articles.summary: AlzeCure has entered an out-licensing and collaboration agreement with Danish biotech QuantumCell ApS valued at more than $2.2 billion, excluding royalty payments. The deal grants QuantumCell global rights to AlzeCure’s NeuroRestore platform, including the clinical-stage candidate ACD856. The Swedish biotech will receive a total upfront payment of $12 million, of which $5 million is a […] The post AlzeCure signs $2.2b deal with QuantumCell to advance Alzheimer’s platform appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

The company’s chairman and chief executive officer, Paul Y. articles.title: United Therapeutics buys Thymmune Therapeutics for $140m
articles.summary: United Therapeutics has acquired Thymmune Therapeutics, a privately held preclinical biotechnology company developing regenerative thymic cell therapies, in a transaction announced on 2 July 2026. The agreement provides $140 million in cash, subject to post-closing adjustments, plus potential earn-out payments of up to $160 million tied to clinical and regulatory milestones through 2031. Thymmune’s platform […] The post United Therapeutics buys Thymmune Therapeutics for $140m appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Q&A: How camera-equipped homing pigeons could improve robotic vision in flight
articles.summary: Contrary to common assumptions, pigeons do not lock their eyes in place during flight. Instead, they make slow, subtle eye movements that may help them gather more information about their surroundings.

articles.title: Quantum computing in transition
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03233-x Hybrid quantum–classical computers will achieve quantum advantage in biotechnology, bridging the time until a full quantum computer becomes available.

articles.title: Mini monitor measures artificial heartbeat
articles.summary: An international team, including the University of Tokyo, has created a sensor inspired by the lateral line in fish—their "sixth sense" organ—which measures the pulse of lab-grown 3D heart tissue (cardiac organoids). The device, called a biomechanical well plate, looks like a small white box containing four liquid-filled wells.

articles.title: Wild yeast discovery enables non-GM brewing of ornithine-enriched craft beer
articles.summary: As consumer interest grows in foods and beverages with added nutritional value, brewers are exploring ways to improve fermentation itself rather than relying on post-production additives. Ornithine, a naturally occurring amino acid involved in several biological processes, has attracted attention as a promising ingredient for value-added products. However, increasing ornithine production in brewing yeast is difficult because the metabolic pathway is tightly regulated, making conventional improvement strategies challenging.

articles.title: Trained AI outperforms biologists at spotting salmon lice
articles.summary: Researchers have taken over 120,000 images of salmon lice larvae in seawater and used them to train AI models. The models were much faster and more accurate than experienced biologists at identifying the parasites that feed on the skin and blood of salmonids.

articles.title: Small-molecule switches put therapeutic CRISPR editing under on-demand control in living tissues
articles.summary: In a study published in Science Translational Medicine, a team of researchers led by Dr. Wang Yu from the Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology of the Chinese Academy of Sciences developed PRINCE and Little Prince, dual small-molecule-controlled genome editing systems that allow CRISPR activity to be switched on by drug inducers and kept largely silent in their absence.

articles.title: Giant wheat starch granules—a leap forward in biological engineering with potential benefits for diet, manufacturing
articles.summary: Scientists have grown wheat containing supersized starch granules—a leap forward in biological engineering with potential benefits for our daily diets and a raft of industrial applications.

articles.title: How a new fungal genome-editing tool could open fresh paths to cancer treatments
articles.summary: Researchers have spent decades—and billions of dollars—sequencing animal and crop genomes, but fungi have historically been the forgotten middle child of genomics, only noticed when they're ruining bread or colonizing toes.

articles.title: UN agency moves Stellar blockchain payment initiative beyond pilot stage
articles.summary: The UN Development Programme said blockchain payment pilots in five countries cut costs, improved resilience and will now inform wider use across its humanitarian and development programs.

articles.title: Ethereum developers embrace Vitalik Buterin's long-term vision but urge quicker execution
articles.summary: The "Lean Ethereum" strawmap, updated earlier this week, has gained support from many of the network's researchers, though several say the biggest challenge won't be deciding what to build, but how quickly it can happen.
